hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n2264\t\t\tETag:          etag,\n2265\t\t\tKeyID:         state.KeyID,\n2266\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n2267\t\n2268\t\t// Add multipart flag to indicate HMAC table is external\n2269\t\tmeta[\"x-amz-meta-armor-multipart\"] = \"true\"\n2270\t\n2271\t\t// Update metadata via CopyObject\n2272\t\tif err := h.backend.Copy(ctx, bucket, key, bucket, key, meta, true); err != nil {\n2273\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to update metadata: %v\", err), 500)\n2274\t\t\treturn\n2275\t\t}\n2276\t\n2277\t\t// Clean up multipart state\n2278\t\tmanager.DeleteState(ctx, uploadID)\n2279\t\n2280\t\t// Record in manifest for fast metadata lookup (async B2 persistence)\n2281\t\tif h.manifest != nil {\n2282\t\t\th.manifest.RecordPut(bucket, key, totalPlaintextSize, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), state.IV, state.WrappedDEK, state.BlockSize, state.ContentType, etag)\n2283\t\t}\n2284\t\n2285\t\t// Record provenance for the multipart upload\n2286\t\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(key) {\n2287\t\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, key, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), \"multipart\")\n2288\t\t}\n2289\t\n2290\t\t// Build XML response\n2291\t\ttype CompleteMultipartUploadResult struct {\n2292\t\t\tXMLName  xml.Name    string   `xml:\"Key\"`\n2297\t\t\tETag     string   `xml:\"ETag\"`\n2298\t\t}\n2299\t\n2300\t\tresult := CompleteMultipartUploadResult{\n2301\t\t\tXmlns:    \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n2302\t\t\tLocation: fmt.Sprintf(\"/%s/%s\", bucket, key),\n2303\t\t\tBucket:   bucket,\n2304\t\t\tKey:      key,\n2305\t\t\tETag: B2.\n2322\tfunc (h *Handlers) AbortMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n2323\t\tctx := r.Context()\n2324\t\n2325\t\t// Load multipart state to verify it exists\n2326\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n2327\t\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n2328\t\tif err != nil {\n2329\t\t\th.writeError(w, sizes.\n2353\tfunc (h *Handlers) ListParts(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n2354\t\tctx := r.Context()\n2355\t\n2356\t\t// Load multipart state to get plaintext configured or the key doesn't start with the prefix, returns the key unchanged.\n2799\tfunc (h *Handlers) stripPrefix(key string) string {\n2800\t\tif h.config.Prefix == \"\" {\n2801\t\t\treturn {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c00fef9d1bac4c2f98b0c12e","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage validate\n2\t\n3\t/*\n4\tPackage validate provides a standardized, extensible validation error format for HTTP API testing.\n5\t\n6\tThis package defines the ValidationError data structure, which offers a comprehensive, machine-readable\n7\tformat for representing validation failures across different types of API validations (HTTP status codes,\n8\terror message patterns, content types, response structures, CORS headers, and more).\n9\t\n10\t# Validation Error Format\n11\t\n12\tThe ValidationError struct is the core of this package's error reporting system. It follows these design\n13\tprinciples:\n14\t\n15\t  - Serializable: Full JSON marshaling/unmarshaling support via struct tags\n16\t  - Machine-readable: Clear field names suitable for automated processing and log aggregation\n17\t  - Human-readable: Descriptive messages with helpful suggestions for debugging\n18\t  - Extensible: Optional fields provide additional context without breaking existing code\n19\t\n20\t# Required vs Optional Fields\n21\t\n22\tRequired fields that must be set for every ValidationError:\n23\t  - ErrorType: The validation category (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n24\t  - Message: A concise, human-readable description of the validation failure\n25\t\n26\tOptional fields that provide additional context:\n27\t  - Expected/Actual: The values that were expected and actually received\n28\t  - Context: Where/when the validation occurred (e.g., endpoint, operation type)\n29\t  - FieldName: Specific field where the error was found (e.g., in response body JSON)\n30\t  - Location: Position information (e.g., \"line 5\", \"field 'user.email'\")\n31\t  - RelatedFields: Other fields related to this error for additional context\n32\t  - PatternDetails: Information about regex pattern matching failures\n33\t  - RangeInfo: Range boundaries for range-based validations (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\")\n34\t  - ValidationDetails: Additional validation-specific information as a list\n35\t  - ResponseSnippet: Truncated excerpt from the response for debugging\n36\t  - Suggestions: Actionable recommendations for resolving the error\n37\t\n38\t# Creating Validation Errors\n39\t\n40\tThis package provides several ways to create ValidationError instances:\n41\t\n42\t  1. Direct struct initialization: Create ValidationError directly for full control\n43\t  2. ValidationFormatter builder: Use the builder pattern for ergonomic, fluent construction\n44\t  3. Convenience functions: Use pre-built functions like FormatStatusCodeError for common cases\n45\t  4. Custom validation: Use FormatCustomValidationError with FormatOption for advanced scenarios\n46\t\n47\tExample usage:\n48\t\n49\t\t// Using the ValidationFormatter builder\n50\t\terr := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n51\t\t\tWithExpected(200).\n52\t\t\tWithActual(404).\n53\t\t\tWithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n54\t\t\tFormat()\n55\t\t// Output:\n56\t\t// status_code validation failed\n57\t\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n58\t\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n59\t\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n60\t\t//   Suggestions:\n61\t\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n62\t\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n63\t\n64\t\t// Using a convenience function\n65\t\terr := FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n66\t\n67\t# Error Interface\n68\t\n69\tValidationError implements the error interface, producing detailed, multi-line error messages\n70\tthat include all relevant validation information. The Error() method formats the output\n71\twith expected/actual values, context, field information, and suggestions for resolution.\n72\t\n73\t# Serialization\n74\t\n75\tValidationError includes JSON struct tags for all fields, enabling easy serialization:\n76\t\n77\t\terr := ValidationError{ErrorType: \"status_code\", Message: \"test\"}\n78\t\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(err)\n79\t\t// Output: {\"error_type\":\"status_code\",\"message\":\"test\"}\n80\t\n81\tThe ToMap() method provides flexible map[string]interface{} conversion for dynamic\n82\tmanipulation before serialization.\n83\t\n84\t# Validation\n85\t\n86\tThe Validate() method checks that a ValidationError is properly structured with\n87\trequired fields populated. Use this to catch programming errors early:\n88\t\n89\t\terr := ValidationError{ErrorType: \"status_code\", Message: \"test\"}\n90\t\tif err := err.Validate(); err != nil {\n91\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"Invalid error structure: %v\", err)\n92\t\t}\n93\t*/\n94\t\n95\timport (\n96\t\t\"fmt\"\n97\t\t\"strings\"\n98\t)\n99\t\n100\t// =============================================================================\n101\t// HELPER TYPES\n102\t// =============================================================================\n103\t\n104\t// ValidationErrorData is a simplified, serialization-friendly version of ValidationError.\n105\t// It provides a consistent format for external communication and API responses.\n106\t// This type is designed to be easily marshaled to JSON and consumed by external systems.\n107\t//\n108\t// Relationship to ValidationError:\n109\t// - ValidationError is the internal, comprehensive error structure used by the validate package\n110\t// - ValidationErrorData is the external, simplified format returned to API clients\n111\t// - Use ToValidationErrorData() to convert ValidationError to ValidationErrorData\n112\t//\n113\t// # Required Fields\n114\t//\n115\t// The MessageType, ErrorType, and Message fields are required for all ValidationErrorData.\n116\t//\n117\t// # Optional Fields\n118\t//\n119\t// All other fields are optional and should be populated based on the specific validation scenario.\n120\t//\n121\t// Example usage:\n122\t//\n123\t//\tdata := ValidationErrorData{\n124\t//\t    MessageType:  \"validation_failed\",\n125\t//\t    ErrorType:    \"status_code\",\n126\t//\t    Message:      \"Expected status code 200 but got 404\",\n127\t//\t    Context:      \"GET /api/users/123\",\n128\t//\t    Expected:     200,\n129\t//\t    Actual:       404,\n130\t//\t    Suggestions:  []string{\"Check the endpoint URL\", \"Verify resource exists\"},\n131\t//\t}\n132\t//\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(data)\n133\ttype ValidationErrorData struct {\n134\t\t// MessageType is a fixed identifier for this type of error message.\n135\t\t// Always \"validation_failed\" for ValidationErrorData instances.\n136\t\t// This field allows clients to quickly identify the error category.\n137\t\tMessageType string `json:\"message_type\"`\n138\t\n139\t\t// ErrorType is the specific validation category that failed.\n140\t\t// Common values include: \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\",\n141\t\t// \"status_code_range\", \"cors_headers\", \"response_structure\".\n142\t\t// Use the ErrorType* constants for type-safe error type specification.\n143\t\tErrorType string `json:\"error_type\"`\n144\t\n145\t\t// Message is a human-readable description of what went wrong.\n146\t\t// This should be a clear, actionable message that explains the validation failure.\n147\t\tMessage string `json:\"message\"`\n148\t\n149\t\t// Context provides additional information about where or when the validation occurred.\n150\t\t// Examples: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"OAuth token validation\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n151\t\tContext string `json:\"context,omitempty\"`\n152\t\n153\t\t// Expected contains the value that was expected during validation.\n154\t\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n155\t\tExpected interface{} `json:\"expected,omitempty\"`\n156\t\n157\t\t// Actual contains the value that was actually received during validation.\n158\t\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n159\t\tActual interface{} `json:\"actual,omitempty\"`\n160\t\n161\t\t// FieldName specifies the field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\").\n162\t\t// This is particularly useful for error message validation in response bodies.\n163\t\tFieldName string `json:\"field_name,omitempty\"`\n164\t\n165\t\t// Location specifies where in the input the error occurred.\n166\t\t// Examples: \"line 5\", \"field 'user.email'\", \"position 123\", \"header 'Authorization'\"\n167\t\tLocation string `json:\"location,omitempty\"`\n168\t\n169\t\t// RelatedFields lists fields related to this error for additional context.\n170\t\t// Examples: [\"email\", \"email_confirmation\"], [\"access_token\", \"refresh_token\"]\n171\t\tRelatedFields []string `json:\"related_fields,omitempty\"`\n172\t\n173\t\t// PatternDetails contains information about pattern matching failures.\n174\t\t// Example: \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\"\n175\t\tPatternDetails string `json:\"pattern_details,omitempty\"`\n176\t\n177\t\t// RangeInfo specifies range boundaries for range validation failures.\n178\t\t// Example: \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\"\n179\t\tRangeInfo string `json:\"range_info,omitempty\"`\n180\t\n181\t\t// ValidationDetails contains additional validation-specific information.\n182\t\t// This can include multiple details as a list of strings for comprehensive error reporting.\n183\t\tValidationDetails []string `json:\"validation_details,omitempty\"`\n184\t\n185\t\t// ResponseSnippet is a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\n186\t\t// This should be limited to a reasonable length (e.g., 200 characters) to keep\n187\t\t// error messages readable while providing useful context.\n188\t\tResponseSnippet string `json:\"response_snippet,omitempty\"`\n189\t\n190\t\t// Suggestions provides actionable recommendations for resolving the validation error.\n191\t\t// When provided, suggestions should be specific and actionable.\n192\t\tSuggestions []string `json:\"suggestions,omitempty\"`\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// Error implements the error interface for ValidationErrorData.\n196\t// It returns the Message field for compatibility with error handling patterns.\n197\t//\n198\t// Example usage:\n199\t//\n200\t//\tdata := ValidationErrorData{Message: \"Validation failed\"}\n201\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Error: %v\", data)\n202\tfunc (ve ValidationErrorData) Error() string {\n203\t\treturn ve.Message\n204\t}\n205\t\n206\t// ToValidationErrorData converts a ValidationError to ValidationErrorData format.\n207\t// This function creates a simplified, serialization-friendly version of the validation error\n208\t// suitable for external communication and API responses.\n209\t//\n210\t// The conversion maps ValidationError fields to ValidationErrorData fields,\n211\t// with the following special handling:\n212\t// - ErrorType is directly copied\n213\t// - Message is generated if empty\n214\t// - MessageType is always set to \"validation_failed\"\n215\t// - All other fields are preserved if present\n216\t//\n217\t// Parameters:\n218\t//   - ve: The ValidationError to convert\n219\t//\n220\t// Returns a ValidationErrorData instance with populated fields.\n221\t//\n222\t// Example usage:\n223\t//\n224\t//\tve := ValidationError{\n225\t//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code\",\n226\t//\t    Expected:  200,\n227\t//\t    Actual:    404,\n228\t//\t    Context:   \"GET /api/users\",\n229\t//\t}\n230\t//\tdata := ToValidationErrorData(ve)\n231\t//\t// data.MessageType = \"validation_failed\"\n232\t//\t// data.ErrorType = \"status_code\"\n233\t//\t// data.Context = \"GET /api/users\"\n234\tfunc ToValidationErrorData(ve ValidationError) ValidationErrorData {\n235\t\t// Generate message if not provided\n236\t\tmessage := ve.Message\n237\t\tif message == \"\" {\n238\t\t\t// Generate detailed message with expected/actual values\n239\t\t\tmessage = generateValidationMessage(ve.ErrorType, ve.Expected, ve.Actual)\n240\t\t}\n241\t\n242\t\tdata := ValidationErrorData{\n243\t\t\tMessageType:  \"validation_failed\",\n244\t\t\tErrorType:    ve.ErrorType,\n245\t\t\tMessage:      message,\n246\t\t\tContext:      ve.Context,\n247\t\t\tExpected:     ve.Expected,\n248\t\t\tActual:       ve.Actual,\n249\t\t\tFieldName:    ve.FieldName,\n250\t\t\tLocation:     ve.Location,\n251\t\t\tRelatedFields:     ve.RelatedFields,\n252\t\t\tPatternDetails:    ve.PatternDetails,\n253\t\t\tRangeInfo:         ve.RangeInfo,\n254\t\t\tValidationDetails: ve.ValidationDetails,\n255\t\t\tResponseSnippet:   ve.ResponseSnippet,\n256\t\t\tSuggestions:       ve.Suggestions,\n257\t\t}\n258\t\n259\t\treturn data\n260\t}\n261\t\n262\t// generateValidationMessage creates a detailed validation message with expected/actual values.\n263\tfunc generateValidationMessage(errorType string, expected, actual interface{}) string {\n264\t\tvar parts []string\n265\t\n266\t\t// Add expected value details\n267\t\tif expected != nil {\n268\t\t\tswitch exp := expected.(type) {\n269\t\t\tcase int:\n270\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected %d (%s)\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp)))\n271\t\t\tcase []int:\n272\t\t\t\texpectedStr := \"one of [\"\n273\t\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n274\t\t\t\t\tif i > 0 {\n275\t\t\t\t\t\texpectedStr += \", \"\n276\t\t\t\t\t}\n277\t\t\t\t\texpectedStr += fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code))\n278\t\t\t\t}\n279\t\t\t\texpectedStr += \"]\"\n280\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, expectedStr)\n281\t\t\tcase string:\n282\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected '%s'\", exp))\n283\t\t\tdefault:\n284\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected %v\", exp))\n285\t\t\t}\n286\t\t}\n287\t\n288\t\t// Add actual value details\n289\t\tif actual != nil {\n290\t\t\tswitch act := actual.(type) {\n291\t\t\tcase int:\n292\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"but got %d (%s)\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act)))\n293\t\t\tcase string:\n294\t\t\t\tif len(act) > 50 {\n295\t\t\t\t\tact = act[:50] + \"...\"\n296\t\t\t\t}\n297\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"but got '%s'\", act))\n298\t\t\tdefault:\n299\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"but got %v\", act))\n300\t\t\t}\n301\t\t}\n302\t\n303\t\t// Combine parts\n304\t\tif len(parts) > 0 {\n305\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed: %s\", errorType, strings.Join(parts, \" \"))\n306\t\t}\n307\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", errorType)\n308\t}\n309\t\n310\t// ValidationError is the core data structure for validation error representation.\n311\t// It provides a standardized format for validation errors across different\n312\t// validation types (status codes, error messages, content types, etc.).\n313\t//\n314\t// This structure is designed to be:\n315\t// - Serializable: Includes JSON tags for marshaling/unmarshaling\n316\t// - Machine-readable: Clear field names for automated processing\n317\t// - Human-readable: Descriptive messages for debugging\n318\t// - Extensible: Optional fields for additional context\n319\t//\n320\t// # Required Fields\n321\t//\n322\t// The Message and ErrorType fields are required for all validation errors.\n323\t//\n324\t// # Optional Fields\n325\t//\n326\t// All other fields are optional and should be populated based on the\n327\t// specific validation scenario.\n328\t//\n329\t// Example usage:\n330\t//\n331\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n332\t//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code_mismatch\",\n333\t//\t    Message:   \"Expected status code 200 but got 404\",\n334\t//\t    Expected:  200,\n335\t//\t    Actual:    404,\n336\t//\t    Context:   \"GET /api/users/123\",\n337\t//\t}\n338\t//\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(err)\n339\ttype ValidationError struct {\n340\t\t// ErrorType is the specific validation category that failed.\n341\t\t// This field identifies what kind of validation was performed and failed.\n342\t\t// Common values include: \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\",\n343\t\t// \"status_code_range\", \"cors_headers\", \"response_structure\".\n344\t\t//\n345\t\t// This field is required for all validation errors.\n346\t\tErrorType string `json:\"error_type\"`\n347\t\n348\t\t// Message is a human-readable description of what went wrong.\n349\t\t// This should be a clear, actionable message that explains the validation failure.\n350\t\t// The message should be concise but informative enough for debugging.\n351\t\t//\n352\t\t// This field is required for all validation errors.\n353\t\tMessage string `json:\"message\"`\n354\t\n355\t\t// Context provides additional information about where or when the validation occurred.\n356\t\t// This can include endpoint names, operation types, or other contextual details.\n357\t\t// Examples: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"OAuth token validation\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n358\t\t// This field is optional and can be empty if no context is available.\n359\t\tContext string `json:\"context,omitempty\"`\n360\t\n361\t\t// Expected contains the value that was expected during validation.\n362\t\t// This field is optional and may not be relevant for all validation types.\n363\t\t// When present, it helps identify the discrepancy between expected and actual values.\n364\t\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n365\t\tExpected interface{} `json:\"expected,omitempty\"`\n366\t\n367\t\t// Actual contains the value that was actually received during validation.\n368\t\t// This field is optional and may not be relevant for all validation types.\n369\t\t// When present, it shows the specific value that caused the validation failure.\n370\t\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n371\t\tActual interface{} `json:\"actual,omitempty\"`\n372\t\n373\t\t// FieldName specifies the field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\").\n374\t\t// This is particularly useful for error message validation in response bodies.\n375\t\t// This field is optional.\n376\t\tFieldName string `json:\"field_name,omitempty\"`\n377\t\n378\t\t// Location specifies where in the input the error occurred.\n379\t\t// Examples: \"line 5\", \"field 'user.email'\", \"position 123\", \"header 'Authorization'\"\n380\t\t// This field is optional.\n381\t\tLocation string `json:\"location,omitempty\"`\n382\t\n383\t\t// RelatedFields lists fields related to this error for additional context.\n384\t\t// Examples: [\"email\", \"email_confirmation\"], [\"access_token\", \"refresh_token\"]\n385\t\t// This field is optional.\n386\t\tRelatedFields []string `json:\"related_fields,omitempty\"`\n387\t\n388\t\t// PatternDetails contains information about pattern matching failures.\n389\t\t// This is useful when validating error messages against regex patterns.\n390\t\t// Example: \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\"\n391\t\t// This field is optional.\n392\t\tPatternDetails string `json:\"pattern_details,omitempty\"`\n393\t\n394\t\t// RangeInfo specifies range boundaries for range validation failures.\n395\t\t// Example: \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\"\n396\t\t// This field is optional.\n397\t\tRangeInfo string `json:\"range_info,omitempty\"`\n398\t\n399\t\t// ValidationDetails contains additional validation-specific information.\n400\t\t// This can include multiple details as a list of strings for comprehensive error reporting.\n401\t\t// This field is optional.\n402\t\tValidationDetails []string `json:\"validation_details,omitempty\"`\n403\t\n404\t\t// ResponseSnippet is a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\n405\t\t// This should be limited to a reasonable length (e.g., 200 characters) to keep\n406\t\t// error messages readable while providing useful context.\n407\t\t// This field is optional.\n408\t\tResponseSnippet string `json:\"response_snippet,omitempty\"`\n409\t\n410\t\t// Suggestions provides actionable recommendations for resolving the validation error.\n411\t\t// This field is optional and contains hints or steps to fix the issue.\n412\t\t// When provided, suggestions should be specific and actionable.\n413\t\tSuggestions []string `json:\"suggestions,omitempty\"`\n414\t}\n415\t\n416\t// Error implements the error interface for ValidationError.\n417\t// It returns a detailed, multi-line error message that includes all relevant\n418\t// validation information for debugging and resolution.\n419\t//\n420\t// The output includes:\n421\t// - The validation type\n422\t// - Expected vs actual values\n423\t// - Context (if provided)\n424\t// - Field name (if provided)\n425\t// - Location (if provided)\n426\t// - Related fields (if provided)\n427\t// - Pattern details (if provided)\n428\t// - Range info (if provided)\n429\t// - Validation details (if provided)\n430\t// - Response snippet (if provided)\n431\t// - Suggestions for fixing the issue\n432\t//\n433\t// Example usage:\n434\t//\n435\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n436\t//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code\",\n437\t//\t    Message:   \"Expected 200 but got 404\",\n438\t//\t    Expected:  200,\n439\t//\t    Actual:    404,\n440\t//\t}\n441\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\n442\tfunc (ve ValidationError) Error() string {\n443\t\tvar b strings.Builder\n444\t\n445\t\t// Use the Message field as the first line if it's different from the default\n446\t\tif ve.Message != \"\" && !strings.HasPrefix(ve.Message, ve.ErrorType+\" validation failed\") {\n447\t\t\tb.WriteString(ve.Message)\n448\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n449\t\t} else {\n450\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\\n\", ve.ErrorType))\n451\t\t}\n452\t\n453\t\t// Expected vs Actual\n454\t\tif ve.Expected != nil || ve.Actual != nil {\n455\t\t\tswitch exp := ve.Expected.(type) {\n456\t\t\tcase int:\n457\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %d (%s)\\n\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp)))\n458\t\t\tcase []int:\n459\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Expected: one of [\")\n460\t\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n461\t\t\t\t\tif i > 0 {\n462\t\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\", \")\n463\t\t\t\t\t}\n464\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code)))\n465\t\t\t\t}\n466\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"]\\n\")\n467\t\t\tcase string:\n468\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %s\\n\", exp))\n469\t\t\tdefault:\n470\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %v\\n\", exp))\n471\t\t\t}\n472\t\n473\t\t\t// Actual value\n474\t\t\tswitch act := ve.Actual.(type) {\n475\t\t\tcase int:\n476\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act)))\n477\t\t\tcase string:\n478\t\t\t\tif len(act) > 100 {\n479\t\t\t\t\tact = act[:100] + \"...\"\n480\t\t\t\t}\n481\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %s\\n\", act))\n482\t\t\tdefault:\n483\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %v\\n\", act))\n484\t\t\t}\n485\t\t}\n486\t\n487\t\t// Context\n488\t\tif ve.Context != \"\" {\n489\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", ve.Context))\n490\t\t}\n491\t\n492\t\t// Field name\n493\t\tif ve.FieldName != \"\" {\n494\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field:    %s\\n\", ve.FieldName))\n495\t\t}\n496\t\n497\t\t// Location\n498\t\tif ve.Location != \"\" {\n499\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: %s\\n\", ve.Location))\n500\t\t}\n501\t\n502\t\t// Related fields\n503\t\tif len(ve.RelatedFields) > 0 {\n504\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Related fields: [\")\n505\t\t\tfor i, field := range ve.RelatedFields {\n506\t\t\t\tif i > 0 {\n507\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\", \")\n508\t\t\t\t}\n509\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(field)\n510\t\t\t}\n511\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"]\\n\")\n512\t\t}\n513\t\n514\t\t// Pattern details\n515\t\tif ve.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n516\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Pattern:  %s\\n\", ve.PatternDetails))\n517\t\t}\n518\t\n519\t\t// Range info\n520\t\tif ve.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n521\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Range:    %s\\n\", ve.RangeInfo))\n522\t\t}\n523\t\n524\t\t// Validation details\n525\t\tif len(ve.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n526\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Details:\\n\")\n527\t\t\tfor _, detail := range ve.ValidationDetails {\n528\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"    - %s\\n\", detail))\n529\t\t\t}\n530\t\t}\n531\t\n532\t\t// Response snippet\n533\t\tif ve.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n534\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Response: %s\\n\", ve.ResponseSnippet))\n535\t\t}\n536\t\n537\t\t// Suggestions\n538\t\tif len(ve.Suggestions) > 0 {\n539\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Suggestions:\\n\")\n540\t\t\tfor _, suggestion := range ve.Suggestions {\n541\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"    - %s\\n\", suggestion))\n542\t\t\t}\n543\t\t}\n544\t\n545\t\treturn b.String()\n546\t}\n547\t\n548\t// getStatusCodeDescription returns a human-readable description for a status code.\n549\t// This is a lightweight version for error formatting.\n550\tfunc getStatusCodeDescription(code int) string {\n551\t\tdescriptions := map[int]string{\n552\t\t\t100: \"Continue\", 101: \"Switching Protocols\", 102: \"Processing\",\n553\t\t\t200: \"OK\", 201: \"Created\", 202: \"Accepted\", 203: \"Non-Authoritative Information\",\n554\t\t\t204: \"No Content\", 205: \"Reset Content\", 206: \"Partial Content\",\n555\t\t\t300: \"Multiple Choices\", 301: \"Moved Permanently\", 302: \"Found\",\n556\t\t\t303: \"See Other\", 304: \"Not Modified\", 307: \"Temporary Redirect\",\n557\t\t\t308: \"Permanent Redirect\",\n558\t\t\t400: \"Bad Request\", 401: \"Unauthorized\", 402: \"Payment Required\",\n559\t\t\t403: \"Forbidden\", 404: \"Not Found\", 405: \"Method Not Allowed\",\n560\t\t\t406: \"Not Acceptable\", 407: \"Proxy Authentication Required\",\n561\t\t\t408: \"Request Timeout\", 409: \"Conflict\", 410: \"Gone\",\n562\t\t\t411: \"Length Required\", 412: \"Precondition Failed\",\n563\t\t\t413: \"Payload Too Large\", 414: \"URI Too Long\",\n564\t\t\t415: \"Unsupported Media Type\", 416: \"Range Not Satisfiable\",\n565\t\t\t417: \"Expectation Failed\", 418: \"I'm a teapot\",\n566\t\t\t422: \"Unprocessable Entity\", 423: \"Locked\", 424: \"Failed Dependency\",\n567\t\t\t426: \"Upgrade Required\", 428: \"Precondition Required\",\n568\t\t\t429: \"Too Many Requests\", 431: \"Request Header Fields Too Large\",\n569\t\t\t451: \"Unavailable For Legal Reasons\",\n570\t\t\t500: \"Internal Server Error\", 501: \"Not Implemented\",\n571\t\t\t502: \"Bad Gateway\", 503: \"Service Unavailable\",\n572\t\t\t504: \"Gateway Timeout\", 505: \"HTTP Version Not Supported\",\n573\t\t\t506: \"Variant Also Negotiates\", 507: \"Insufficient Storage\",\n574\t\t\t508: \"Loop Detected\", 510: \"Not Extended\",\n575\t\t\t511: \"Network Authentication Required\",\n576\t\t}\n577\t\n578\t\tif desc, ok := descriptions[code]; ok {\n579\t\t\treturn desc\n580\t\t}\n581\t\treturn \"Unknown\"\n582\t}\n583\t\n584\t// ToMap converts the ValidationError to a map[string]interface{} for flexible serialization.\n585\t// This is useful when you need to dynamically manipulate or filter the error data before\n586\t// JSON encoding or when working with systems that require map input.\n587\t//\n588\t// Returns a map containing all non-empty fields from the ValidationError.\n589\t//\n590\t// Example usage:\n591\t//\n592\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n593\t//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code\",\n594\t//\t    Message:   \"Expected 200 but got 404\",\n595\t//\t    Expected:  200,\n596\t//\t    Actual:    404,\n597\t//\t}\n598\t//\tdata := err.ToMap()\n599\t//\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(data)\n600\tfunc (ve ValidationError) ToMap() map[string]interface{} {\n601\t\tresult := map[string]interface{}{\n602\t\t\t\"error_type\": ve.ErrorType,\n603\t\t\t\"message\":    ve.Message,\n604\t\t}\n605\t\n606\t\tif ve.Context != \"\" {\n607\t\t\tresult[\"context\"] = ve.Context\n608\t\t}\n609\t\tif ve.Expected != nil {\n610\t\t\tresult[\"expected\"] = ve.Expected\n611\t\t}\n612\t\tif ve.Actual != nil {\n613\t\t\tresult[\"actual\"] = ve.Actual\n614\t\t}\n615\t\tif ve.FieldName != \"\" {\n616\t\t\tresult[\"field_name\"] = ve.FieldName\n617\t\t}\n618\t\tif ve.Location != \"\" {\n619\t\t\tresult[\"location\"] = uploadID)\\n215\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n216\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n217\\t\\t\\t\\t// Check for CopyObject (has x-amz-copy-source header)\\n218\\t\\t\\t\\tif r.Header.Get(\\\"x-amz-copy-source\\\") != \\\"\\\" {\\n219\\t\\t\\t\\t\\th.CopyObject(w, r, bucket, key)\\n220\\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n221\\t\\t\\t\\t\\th.PutObject(w, r, bucket, key)\\n222\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n223\\t\\t\\t} else if bucket != \\\"\\\" {\\n224\\t\\t\\t\\th.CreateBucket(w, r, bucket)\\n225\\t\\t\\t}\\n226\\t\\tcase http.MethodHead:\\n227\\t\\t\\tif key != \\\"\\\" {\\n228\\t\\t\\t\\th.HeadObject(w, r, bucket, key)\\n229\\t\\t\\t} else if bucket != \\\"\\\" {\\n230\\t\\t\\t\\th.HeadBucket(w, r, bucket)\\n231\\t\\t\\t}\\n232\\t\\tcase http.MethodDelete:\\n233\\t\\t\\t// Handle DeleteBucketLifecycleConfiguration (DELETE ?lifecycle on bucket)\\n234\\t\\t\\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\\\"lifecycle\\\") && key == \\\"\\\" && bucket != \\\"\\\" {\\n235\\t\\t\\t\\th.DeleteBucketLifecycleConfiguration(w, r, bucket)\\n236\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n237\\t\\t\\t}\\n238\\t\\t\\t// Handle AbortMultipartUpload (DELETE ?uploadId on object)\\n239\\t\\t\\tif uploadID := r.URL.Query().Get(\\\"uploadId\\\"); uploadID != \\\"\\\" && key != \\\"\\\" {\\n240\\t\\t\\t\\th.AbortMultipartUpload(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\\n241\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n242\\t\\t\\t}\\n243\\t\\t\\tif key != \\\"\\\" {\\n244\\t\\t\\t\\th.DeleteObject(w, r, bucket, key)\\n245\\t\\t\\t} else if bucket != \\\"\\\" {\\n246\\t\\t\\t\\th.DeleteBucket(w, r, bucket)\\n247\\t\\t\\t}\\n248\\t\\tcase http.MethodPost:\\n249\\t\\t\\t// Handle multipart upload operations\\n250\\t\\t\\tuploadID := r.URL.Query().Get(\\\"uploadId\\\")\\n251\\t\\t\\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\\\"uploads\\\") {\\n252\\t\\t\\t\\th.CreateMultipartUpload(w, r, bucket, key)\\n253\\t\\t\\t} else if uploadID != \\\"\\\" {\\n254\\t\\t\\t\\tif r.URL.Query().Get(\\\"partNumber\\\") != \\\"\\\" {\\n255\\t\\t\\t\\t\\th.UploadPart(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\\n256\\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n257\\t\\t\\t\\t\\th.CompleteMultipartUpload(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\\n258\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n259\\t\\t\\t} else if r.URL.Query().Has(\\\"delete\\\") {\\n260\\t\\t\\t\\t// DeleteObjects (bulk delete) - uses POST with ?delete query param\\n261\\t\\t\\t\\th.DeleteObjects(w, r, bucket)\\n262\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n263\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InvalidRequest\\\", \\\"Unsupported POST operation\\\", 400)\\n264\\t\\t\\t}\\n265\\t\\tdefault:\\n266\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"MethodNotAllowed\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Method %s not allowed\\\", r.Method), 405)\\n267\\t\\t}\\n268\\t}\\n269\\t\\n270\\t// PutObject handles S3 PutObject with encryption.\\n271\\t// For small files (<10MB), it buffers in memory.\\n272\\t// For larger files, it uses streaming encryption via temp files to avoid\\n273\\t// loading the entire file into memory.\\n274\\tfunc (h *Handlers) PutObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\\n275\\t\\tctx := r.Context()\\n276\\t\\n277\\t\\t// Check Content-Length header\\n278\\t\\tcontentLength := r.ContentLength\\n279\\t\\tstreamingThreshold := int64(10 * 1024 * 1024) // 10MB threshold\\n280\\t\\n281\\t\\t// Use streaming for large files or unknown size\\n282\\t\\tuseStreaming := contentLength < 0 || contentLength > streamingThreshold\\n283\\t\\n284\\t\\tif useStreaming {\\n285\\t\\t\\th.putObjectStreaming(ctx, w, r, bucket, key)\\n286\\t\\t\\treturn\\n287\\t\\t}\\n288\\t\\n289\\t\\t// Small file: buffer in memory\\n290\\t\\tplaintext, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\\n291\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n292\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to read body: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n293\\t\\t\\treturn\\n294\\t\\t}\\n295\\t\\n296\\t\\tplaintextSize := int64(len(plaintext))\\n297\\t\\n298\\t\\t// Get the appropriate MEK for this object key\\n299\\t\\tmek, keyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(key)\\n300\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n301\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to get encryption key: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n302\\t\\t\\treturn\\n303\\t\\t}\\n304\\t\\n305\\t\\t// Generate DEK and IV\\n306\\t\\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\\n307\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n308\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to generate DEK: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n309\\t\\t\\treturn\\n310\\t\\t}\\n311\\t\\n312\\t\\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\\n313\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n314\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to generate IV: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n315\\t\\t\\treturn\\n316\\t\\t}\\n317\\t\\n318\\t\\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\\n319\\t\\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(mek, dek)\\n320\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n321\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to wrap DEK: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n322\\t\\t\\treturn\\n323\\t\\t}\\n324\\t\\n325\\t\\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\\n326\\t\\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(plaintext)\\n327\\t\\n328\\t\\t// Create envelope header\\n329\\t\\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, plaintextSize, h.config.BlockSize, plaintextSHA)\\n330\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n331\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to create header: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n332\\t\\t\\treturn\\n333\\t\\t}\\n334\\t\\n335\\t\\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\\n336\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n337\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to encode header: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n338\\t\\t\\treturn\\n339\\t\\t}\\n340\\t\\n341\\t\\t// Encrypt data\\n342\\t\\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, h.config.BlockSize)\\n343\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n344\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to create encryptor: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n345\\t\\t\\treturn\\n346\\t\\t}\\n347\\t\\n348\\t\\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(plaintext)\\n349\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n350\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to encrypt: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n351\\t\\t\\treturn\\n352\\t\\t}\\n353\\t\\n354\\t\\t// Build envelope: header + encrypted blocks + HMAC table\\n355\\t\\tenvelopeSize := int64(len(headerBytes)) + int64(len(encrypted)) + int64(len(hmacTable))\\n356\\t\\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, envelopeSize)\\n357\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\\n358\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\\n359\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\\n360\\t\\n361\\t\\t// Compute plaintext ETag (MD5)\\n362\\t\\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(plaintext)\\n363\\t\\n364\\t\\t// Build metadata\\n365\\t\\tcontentType := r.Header.Get(\\\"Content-Type\\\")\\n366\\t\\tif contentType == \\\"\\\" {\\n367\\t\\t\\tcontentType = \\\"application/octet-stream\\\"\\n368\\t\\t}\\n369\\t\\n370\\t\\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\\n371\\t\\t\\tVersion:       1,\\n372\\t\\t\\tBlockSize:     h.config.BlockSize,\\n373\\t\\t\\tPlaintextSize: plaintextSize,\\n374\\t\\t\\tContentType:   contentType,\\n375\\t\\t\\tIV:            iv,\\n376\\t\\t\\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\\n377\\t\\t\\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\\n378\\t\\t\\tETag:          etag,\\n379\\t\\t\\tKeyID:         keyID,\\n380\\t\\t}).ToMetadata()\\n381\\t\\n382\\t\\t// Upload to B2 with prefix applied\\n383\\t\\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\\n384\\t\\tif err := h.backend.Put(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta); err != nil {\\n385\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to upload: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n386\\t\\t\\treturn\\n387\\t\\t}\\n388\\t\\n389\\t\\t// Record in manifest for fast metadata lookup (async B2 persistence)\\n390\\t\\tif h.manifest != nil {\\n391\\t\\t\\th.manifest.RecordPut(bucket, key, plaintextSize, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), iv, wrappedDEK, h.config.BlockSize, contentType, etag)\\n392\\t\\t}\\n393\\t\\n394\\t\\t// Record provenance\\n395\\t\\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(key) {\\n396\\t\\t\\tplaintextSHAHex := hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:])\\n397\\t\\t\\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, key, plaintextSHAHex, \\\"put\\\")\\n398\\t\\t}\\n399\\t\\n400\\t\\t// Invalidate list cache entries covering this key's directory\\n401\\t\\tif h.listCache != nil {\\n402\\t\\t\\th.listCache.InvalidatePrefix(bucket, path.Dir(key)+\\\"/\\\")\\n403\\t\\t}\\n404\\t\\n405\\t\\t// Return ETag\\n406\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"ETag\\\", fmt.Sprintf(`\\\"%s\\\"`, etag))\\n407\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\\n408\\t}\\n409\\t\\n410\\t// putObjectStreaming handles large file uploads with streaming encryption.\\n411\\t// It uses a temp file to avoid loading the entire plaintext into memory.\\n412\\t// The process is:\\n413\\t// 1. Stream request body to temp file while computing SHA-256\\n414\\t// 2. Create envelope header with the computed SHA-256\\n415\\t// 3. Stream from temp file through encryption to B2 via io.Pipe\\n416\\t// 4. Clean up temp file\\n417\\tfunc (h *Handlers) putObjectStreaming(ctx context.Context, w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\\n418\\t\\t// Phase 1: Stream to temp file and compute SHA-256\\n419\\t\\ttmpFile, err := os.CreateTemp(\\\"\\\", \\\"armor-upload-*.tmp\\\")\\n420\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n421\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to create temp file: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n422\\t\\t\\treturn\\n423\\t\\t}\\n424\\t\\ttmpPath := tmpFile.Name()\\n425\\t\\tdefer os.Remove(tmpPath) // Clean up on exit\\n426\\t\\n427\\t\\t// Compute SHA-256 while copying to temp file\\n428\\t\\tplaintextHash := sha256.New()\\n429\\t\\tteeReader := io.TeeReader(r.Body, plaintextHash)\\n430\\t\\n431\\t\\tplaintextSize, err := io.Copy(tmpFile, teeReader)\\n432\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n433\\t\\t\\ttmpFile.Close()\\n434\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to read body: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n435\\t\\t\\treturn\\n436\\t\\t}\\n437\\t\\n438\\t\\t// Get the computed SHA-256\\n439\\t\\tvar plaintextSHA [32]byte\\n440\\t\\tcopy(plaintextSHA[:], plaintextHash.Sum(nil))\\n441\\t\\n442\\t\\t// Seek back to beginning of temp file for reading\\n443\\t\\tif _, err := tmpFile.Seek(0, 0); err != nil {\\n444\\t\\t\\ttmpFile.Close()\\n445\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to seek temp file: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n446\\t\\t\\treturn\\n447\\t\\t}\\n448\\t\\n449\\t\\t// Phase 2: Get encryption keys\\n450\\t\\tmek, keyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(key)\\n451\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n452\\t\\t\\ttmpFile.Close()\\n453\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to get encryption key: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n454\\t\\t\\treturn\\n455\\t\\t}\\n456\\t\\n457\\t\\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\\n458\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n459\\t\\t\\ttmpFile.Close()\\n460\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to generate DEK: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n461\\t\\t\\treturn\\n462\\t\\t}\\n463\\t\\n464\\t\\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\\n465\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n466\\t\\t\\ttmpFile.Close()\\n467\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to generate IV: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n468\\t\\t\\treturn\\n469\\t\\t}\\n470\\t\\n471\\t\\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(mek, dek)\\n472\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n473\\t\\t\\ttmpFile.Close()\\n474\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to wrap DEK: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n475\\t\\t\\treturn\\n476\\t\\t}\\n477\\t\\n478\\t\\t// Create envelope header\\n479\\t\\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, plaintextSize, h.config.BlockSize, plaintextSHA)\\n480\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n481\\t\\t\\ttmpFile.Close()\\n482\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to create header: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n483\\t\\t\\treturn\\n484\\t\\t}\\n485\\t\\n486\\t\\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\\n487\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n488\\t\\t\\ttmpFile.Close()\\n489\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to encode header: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n490\\t\\t\\treturn\\n491\\t\\t}\\n492\\t\\n493\\t\\t// Create encryptor\\n494\\t\\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, h.config.BlockSize)\\n495\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n496\\t\\t\\ttmpFile.Close()\\n497\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to create encryptor: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n498\\t\\t\\treturn\\n499\\t\\t}\\n500\\t\\n501\\t\\t// Calculate envelope size\\n502\\t\\tblockCount := crypto.ComputeBlockCount(plaintextSize, h.config.BlockSize)\\n503\\t\\thmacTableSize := int64(blockCount) * crypto.HMACSize\\n504\\t\\tenvelopeSize := int64(len(headerBytes)) + plaintextSize + hmacTableSize\\n505\\t\\n506\\t\\t// Phase 3: Stream encrypt via io.Pipe\\n507\\t\\tpr, pw := io.Pipe()\\n508\\t\\n509\\t\\t// Start encryption goroutine\\n510\\t\\tencErr := make(chan error, 1)\\n511\\t\\tgo func() {\\n512\\t\\t\\tdefer pw.Close()\\n513\\t\\t\\tdefer close(encErr)\\n514\\t\\n515\\t\\t\\t// Write header\\n516\\t\\t\\tif _, err := pw.Write(headerBytes); err != nil {\\n517\\t\\t\\t\\tencErr <- fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to write header: %w\\\", err)\\n518\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n519\\t\\t\\t}\\n520\\t\\n521\\t\\t\\t// Stream encrypt the plaintext\\n522\\t\\t\\thmacTable, err := encryptor.EncryptStream(tmpFile, pw, plaintextSize)\\n523\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n524\\t\\t\\t\\tencErr <- fmt.Errorf(\\\"encryption failed: %w\\\", err)\\n525\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n526\\t\\t\\t}\\n527\\t\\n528\\t\\t\\t// Write HMAC table\\n529\\t\\t\\tif _, err := pw.Write(hmacTable); err != nil {\\n530\\t\\t\\t\\tencErr <- fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to write HMAC table: %w\\\", err)\\n531\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n532\\t\\t\\t}\\n533\\t\\n534\\t\\t\\tencErr <- nil\\n535\\t\\t}()\\n536\\t\\n537\\t\\t// Build metadata\\n538\\t\\tcontentType := r.Header.Get(\\\"Content-Type\\\")\\n539\\t\\tif contentType == \\\"\\\" {\\n540\\t\\t\\tcontentType = \\\"application/octet-stream\\\"\\n541\\t\\t}\\n542\\t\\n543\\t\\t// Compute ETag while we have the temp file\\n544\\t\\t// We need to read the file again for MD5, but we already have SHA-256\\n545\\t\\t// Use SHA-256 truncated to 16 bytes as ETag for streaming (non-standard but works)\\n546\\t\\tetag := hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:16])\\n547\\t\\n548\\t\\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\\n549\\t\\t\\tVersion:       1,\\n550\\t\\t\\tBlockSize:     h.config.BlockSize,\\n551\\t\\t\\tPlaintextSize: plaintextSize,\\n552\\t\\t\\tContentType:   contentType,\\n553\\t\\t\\tIV:            iv,\\n554\\t\\t\\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\\n555\\t\\t\\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\\n556\\t\\t\\tETag:          etag,\\n557\\t\\t\\tKeyID:         keyID,\\n558\\t\\t}).ToMetadata()\\n559\\t\\n560\\t\\t// Upload to B2 with prefix applied using streaming reader\\n561\\t\\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\\n562\\t\\tif err := h.backend.Put(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, pr, envelopeSize, meta); err != nil {\\n563\\t\\t\\ttmpFile.Close()\\n564\\t\\t\\t// Check if there was an encryption error\\n565\\t\\t\\tselect {\\n566\\t\\t\\tcase encErrVal := <-encErr:\\n567\\t\\t\\t\\tif encErrVal != nil {\\n568\\t\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Encryption error: %v\\\", encErrVal), 500)\\n569\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n570\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n571\\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n572\\t\\t\\t}\\n573\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to upload: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n574\\t\\t\\treturn\\n575\\t\\t}\\n576\\t\\n577\\t\\t// Close temp file\\n578\\t\\ttmpFile.Close()\\n579\\t\\n580\\t\\t// Check for encryption errors\\n581\\t\\tif encErrVal := <-encErr; encErrVal != nil {\\n582\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Encryption error: %v\\\", encErrVal), 500)\\n583\\t\\t\\treturn\\n584\\t\\t}\\n585\\t\\n586\\t\\t// Record in manifest for fast metadata lookup (async B2 persistence)\\n587\\t\\tif h.manifest != nil {\\n588\\t\\t\\th.manifest.RecordPut(bucket, key, plaintextSize, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), iv, wrappedDEK, h.config.BlockSize, contentType, etag)\\n589\\t\\t}\\n590\\t\\n591\\t\\t// Record provenance\\n592\\t\\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(key) {\\n593\\t\\t\\tplaintextSHAHex := hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:])\\n594\\t\\t\\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, key, plaintextSHAHex, \\\"put-streaming\\\")\\n595\\t\\t}\\n596\\t\\n597\\t\\t// Invalidate list cache entries covering this key's directory\\n598\\t\\tif h.listCache != nil {\\n599\\t\\t\\th.listCache.InvalidatePrefix(bucket, path.Dir(key)+\\\"/\\\")\\n600\\t\\t}\\n601\\t\\n602\\t\\t// Return ETag\\n603\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"ETag\\\", fmt.Sprintf(`\\\"%s\\\"`, etag))\\n604\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"X-Armor-Streaming\\\", \\\"true\\\")\\n605\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\\n606\\t}\\n607\\t\\n608\\t// GetObject handles S3 GetObject with decryption and range support.\\n609\\tfunc (h *Handlers) GetObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\\n610\\t\\tctx := r.Context()\\n611\\t\\n612\\t\\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\\n613\\t\\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\\n614\\t\\n615\\t\\t// Get metadata first\\n616\\t\\tinfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey)\\n617\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n618\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"NoSuchKey\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Object not found: %v\\\", err), 404)\\n619\\t\\t\\treturn\\n620\\t\\t}\\n621\\t\\n622\\t\\tif !info.IsARMOREncrypted {\\n623\\t\\t\\t// Check conditional request headers for non-ARMOR objects\\n624\\t\\t\\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, info.ETag, info.LastModified); status != 0 {\\n625\\t\\t\\t\\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\\n626\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"ETag\\\", fmt.Sprintf(`\\\"%s\\\"`, info.ETag))\\n627\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Last-Modified\\\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\\n628\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(status)\\n629\\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n630\\t\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"PreconditionFailed\\\", \\\"Precondition failed\\\", status)\\n631\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n632\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n633\\t\\t\\t}\\n634\\t\\n635\\t\\t\\t// Passthrough for non-ARMOR objects\\n636\\t\\t\\tbody, _, err := h.backend.Get(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey)\\n637\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n638\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to get object: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n639\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n640\\t\\t\\t}\\n641\\t\\t\\tdefer body.Close()\\n642\\t\\n643\\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Content-Length\\\", strconv.FormatInt(info.Size, 10))\\n644\\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", info.ContentType)\\n645\\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"ETag\\\", fmt.Sprintf(`\\\"%s\\\"`, info.ETag))\\n646\\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Last-Modified\\\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\\n647\\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\\n648\\t\\t\\tio.Copy(w, body)\\n649\\t\\t\\treturn\\n650\\t\\t}\\n651\\t\\n652\\t\\t// Parse ARMOR metadata\\n653\\t\\tarmorMeta, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata)\\n654\\t\\tif !ok {\\n655\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", \\\"Failed to parse ARMOR metadata\\\", 500)\\n656\\t\\t\\treturn\\n657\\t\\t}\\n658\\t\\n659\\t\\t// Get the MEK for this object using the key ID from metadata\\n660\\t\\tmek, err := h.keyManager.GetMEKByID(armorMeta.KeyID)\\n661\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n662\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to get decryption key: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n663\\t\\t\\treturn\\n664\\t\\t}\\n665\\t\\n666\\t\\t// Unwrap DEK\\n667\\t\\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(mek, armorMeta.WrappedDEK)\\n668\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n669\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to unwrap DEK: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n670\\t\\t\\treturn\\n671\\t\\t}\\n672\\t\\n673\\t\\t// Create decryptor\\n674\\t\\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(dek, armorMeta.IV, armorMeta.BlockSize)\\n675\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n676\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to create decryptor: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n677\\t\\t\\treturn\\n678\\t\\t}\\n679\\t\\n680\\t\\tplaintextSize := armorMeta.PlaintextSize\\n681\\t\\n682\\t\\t// Check conditional request headers\\n683\\t\\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, armorMeta.ETag, info.LastModified); status != 0 {\\n684\\t\\t\\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\\n685\\t\\t\\t\\t// 304 Not Modified - set headers but no body\\n686\\t\\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"ETag\\\", fmt.Sprintf(`\\\"%s\\\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\\n687\\t\\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Last-Modified\\\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\\n688\\t\\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(status)\\n689\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n690\\t\\t\\t\\t// 412 Precondition Failed\\n691\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"PreconditionFailed\\\", \\\"Precondition failed\\\", status)\\n692\\t\\t\\t}\\n693\\t\\t\\treturn\\n694\\t\\t}\\n695\\t\\n696\\t\\t// Check if this is a multipart object (HMAC table in sidecar, no embedded header)\\n697\\t\\tisMultipart := info.Metadata[\\\"x-amz-meta-armor-multipart\\\"] == \\\"true\\\"\\n698\\t\\n699\\t\\t// Check for range request\\n700\\t\\trangeHeader := r.Header.Get(\\\"Range\\\")\\n701\\t\\tif rangeHeader != \\\"\\\" {\\n702\\t\\t\\th.handleRangeRequest(w, r, bucket, key, decryptor, armorMeta, plaintextSize, info.LastModified, isMultipart)\\n703\\t\\t\\treturn\\n704\\t\\t}\\n705\\t\\n706\\t\\t// Full object download with pipelined stream decryption\\n707\\t\\th.handleFullObjectStream(w, r, bucket, key, decryptor, armorMeta, plaintextSize, info.LastModified, isMultipart)\\n708\\t}\\n709\\t\\n710\\t// handleFullObjectStream handles full object downloads with pipelined stream decryption.\\n711\\t// This uses io.Pipe to decrypt blocks as they stream from Cloudflare, reducing\\n712\\t// time-to-first-byte and memory usage compared to buffering the entire envelope.\\n713\\tfunc (h *Handlers) handleFullObjectStream(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, plaintextSize int64, lastModified time.Time, isMultipart bool) {\\n714\\t\\tctx := r.Context()\\n715\\t\\n716\\t\\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\\n717\\t\\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\\n718\\t\\n719\\t\\tblockSize := armorMeta.BlockSize\\n720\\t\\tblockCount := int(crypto.ComputeBlockCount(plaintextSize, blockSize))\\n721\\t\\n722\\t\\tvar hmacTable []byte\\n723\\t\\tvar dataBody io.ReadCloser\\n724\\t\\tvar streamSize int64\\n725\\t\\tvar header *crypto.EnvelopeHeader\\n726\\t\\n727\\t\\tif isMultipart {\\n728\\t\\t\\t// Multipart object: HMAC table is in sidecar, no embedded header\\n729\\t\\t\\tmanager := data\\n742\\t\\t\\tstreamSize = plaintextSize\\n743\\t\\t\\tdataBody, err = h.backend.GetRange(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, 0, streamSize)\\n744\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n745\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to get object stream: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n746\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n747\\t\\t\\t}\\n748\\t\\t\\tdefer dataBody.Close()\\n749\\t\\t} else {\\n750\\t\\t\\t// Single-PUT object: embedded HMAC table at end of file\\n751\\t\\t\\thmacTableOffset := crypto.HeaderSize + plaintextSize\\n752\\t\\t\\thmacTableSize := int64(blockCount) * crypto.HMACSize\\n753\\t\\t\\tdataSize := plaintextSize\\n754\\t\\n755\\t\\t\\t// 1. Prefetch HMAC table (small range read)\\n756\\t\\t\\thmacBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, hmacTableOffset, hmacTableSize)\\n757\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n758\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to prefetch HMAC table: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n759\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n760\\t\\t\\t}\\n761\\t\\t\\thmacTable, err = io.ReadAll(hmacBody)\\n762\\t\\t\\thmacBody.Close()\\n763\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n764\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to read HMAC table: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n765\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n766\\t\\t\\t}\\n767\\t\\n768\\t\\t\\t// 2. Start streaming data from Cloudflare (header + encrypted blocks, stop before HMAC)\\n769\\t\\t\\tstreamSize = crypto.HeaderSize + dataSize\\n770\\t\\t\\tdataBody, err = h.backend.GetRange(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, 0, streamSize)\\n771\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n772\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to get object stream: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n773\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n774\\t\\t\\t}\\n775\\t\\t\\tdefer dataBody.Close()\\n776\\t\\n777\\t\\t\\t// 3. Read and discard the 64-byte header (single-PUT only)\\n778\\t\\t\\theaderBuf := make([]byte, crypto.HeaderSize)\\n779\\t\\t\\tif _, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, headerBuf); err != nil {\\n780\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to read header: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n781\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n782\\t\\t\\t}\\n783\\t\\n784\\t\\t\\t// Parse header to get plaintext SHA for verification\\n785\\t\\t\\theader, err = crypto.DecodeHeader(headerBuf)\\n786\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n787\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to decode header: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n788\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n789\\t\\t\\t}\\n790\\t\\t}\\n791\\t\\n792\\t\\t// 4. Stream decrypt using io.Pipe\\n802\\t\\tpr, pw := io.Pipe()\\n803\\t\\n804\\t\\t// Start decryption goroutine\\n805\\t\\tgo func() {\\n806\\t\\t\\tdefer pw.Close()\\n807\\t\\n808\\t\\t\\tplaintextHash := sha256.New()\\n809\\t\\t\\tencryptedBuf := make([]byte, blockSize)\\n810\\t\\n811\\t\\t\\tfor blockIndex := 0; blockIndex < blockCount; blockIndex++ {\\n812\\t\\t\\t\\t// Calculate actual block size (last block may be smaller)\\n813\\t\\t\\t\\tremaining := plaintextSize - int64(blockIndex)*int64(blockSize)\\n814\\t\\t\\t\\tactualBlockSize := int(min64(int64(blockSize), remaining))\\n815\\t\\n816\\t\\t\\t\\t// Read encrypted block\\n817\\t\\t\\t\\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:actualBlockSize]\\n818\\t\\t\\t\\tn, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, encryptedBuf)\\n819\\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil && err != io.EOF && err != io.ErrUnexpectedEOF {\\n820\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"read error at block %d: %w\\\", blockIndex, err))\\n821\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n822\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n823\\t\\t\\t\\tif n == 0 {\\n824\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n825\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n826\\t\\t\\t\\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:n]\\n827\\t\\n828\\t\\t\\t\\t// Verify HMAC\\n829\\t\\t\\t\\thmacOffset := blockIndex * crypto.HMACSize\\n830\\t\\t\\t\\tif hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize > len(hmacTable) {\\n831\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"HMAC table too short at block %d\\\", blockIndex))\\n832\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n833\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n834\\t\\t\\t\\texpectedHMAC := hmacTable[hmacOffset : hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize]\\n835\\t\\n836\\t\\t\\t\\tmac := hmac.New(sha256.New, decryptor.HMACKey())\\n837\\t\\t\\t\\tindexBytes := make([]byte, 4)\\n838\\t\\t\\t\\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(indexBytes, uint32(blockIndex))\\n839\\t\\t\\t\\tmac.Write(indexBytes)\\n840\\t\\t\\t\\tmac.Write(encryptedBuf)\\n841\\t\\t\\t\\tcomputed := mac.Sum(nil)\\n842\\t\\n843\\t\\t\\t\\tif !hmac.Equal(computed, expectedHMAC) {\\n844\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"block %d: HMAC verification failed\\\", blockIndex))\\n845\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n846\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n847\\t\\n848\\t\\t\\t\\t// Decrypt block (need to use CTR stream)\\n849\\t\\t\\t\\tdecrypted := make([]byte, n)\\n850\\t\\t\\t\\tctr := makeCounter(armorMeta.IV, uint32(blockIndex))\\n851\\t\\t\\t\\tstream := cipher.NewCTR(decryptor.CipherBlock(), ctr)\\n852\\t\\t\\t\\tstream.XORKeyStream(decrypted, encryptedBuf)\\n853\\t\\n854\\t\\t\\t\\t// Update plaintext hash for verification\\n855\\t\\t\\t\\tplaintextHash.Write(decrypted)\\n856\\t\\n857\\t\\t\\t\\t// Write plaintext to pipe\\n858\\t\\t\\t\\tif _, err := pw.Write(decrypted); err != nil {\\n859\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"write error at block %d: %w\\\", blockIndex, err))\\n860\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n861\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n862\\t\\t\\t}\\n863\\t\\n864\\t\\t\\t// Verify plaintext SHA-256\\n865\\t\\t\\tcomputedSHA := plaintextHash.Sum(nil)\\n866\\t\\t\\tvar expectedSHA []byte\\n867\\t\\t\\tif isMultipart {\\n868\\t\\t\\t\\t// For multipart objects, use SHA from metadata (placeholder until tracked during upload)\\n869\\t\\t\\t\\t// See bf-1v2ehf: multipart currently stores empty hash\\n870\\t\\t\\t\\texpectedSHA, _ = hex.DecodeString(armorMeta.PlaintextSHA)\\n871\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n872\\t\\t\\t\\t// For single-PUT objects, use SHA from header\\n873\\t\\t\\t\\texpectedSHA = header.PlaintextSHA[:]\\n874\\t\\t\\t}\\n875\\t\\t\\tif len(expectedSHA) > 0 && !bytes.Equal(computedSHA, expectedSHA) {\\n876\\t\\t\\t\\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"plaintext SHA-256 mismatch\\\"))\\n877\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n878\\t\\t\\t}\\n879\\t\\t}()\\n880\\t\\n881\\t\\t// Stream plaintext to client\\n882\\t\\tio.Copy(w, pr)\\n883\\t}\\n884\\t\\n885\\t// makeCounter creates a 16-byte counter value from the IV and block index.\\n886\\tfunc makeCounter(iv []byte, blockIndex uint32) []byte {\\n887\\t\\tcounter := make([]byte, 16)\\n888\\t\\tcopy(counter[0:12], iv[0:12])\\n889\\t\\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(counter[12:16], blockIndex)\\n890\\t\\treturn counter\\n891\\t}\\n892\\t\\n893\\tfunc min64(a, b int64) int64 {\\n894\\t\\tif a < b {\\n895\\t\\t\\treturn a\\n896\\t\\t}\\n897\\t\\treturn b\\n898\\t}\\n899\\t\\n900\\t// handleRangeRequest handles range read requests.\\n901\\tfunc (h *Handlers) handleRangeRequest(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, plaintextSize int64, lastModified time.Time, isMultipart bool) {\\n902\\t\\tctx := r.Context()\\n903\\t\\n904\\t\\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\\n905\\t\\tprefixedKey := plaintextSize)\\n910\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n911\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InvalidRange\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Invalid range: %v\\\", err), 400)\\n912\\t\\t\\treturn\\n913\\t\\t}\\n914\\t\\n915\\t\\t// int64(blockSize))\\n964\\t\\n965\\t\\t\\t// Clamp to valid range\\n966\\t\\t\\tblockCount := int(crypto.ComputeBlockCount(plaintextSize, blockSize))\\n967\\t\\t\\tif endBlockIndex >= blockCount prefixedKey, dataOffset, dataLength)\\n981\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n982\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"failed to fetch encrypted blocks: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n983\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n984\\t\\t\\t}\\n985\\t\\t\\tdefer encryptedBody.Close()\\n986\\t\\n987\\t\\t\\tencrypted, err = io.ReadAll(encryptedBody)\\n988\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n989\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"failed to read encrypted blocks: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n990\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n991\\t\\t\\t}\\n992\\t\\t} else {\\n993\\t\\t\\t// Single-PUT object: embedded HMAC table at end of file\\n994\\t\\t\\t// Translate range to encrypted blocks\\n995\\t\\t\\ttranslation, err := crypto.TranslateRange(start, end, {\\n1020\\t\\t\\t\\thmacBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(gctx, bucket, prefixedKey, translation.HMACOffset, translation.HMACLength)\\n1021\\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n1022\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn 500)\\n1035\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n1036\\t\\t\\t}\\n1037\\t\\t}\\n1038\\t\\n1039\\t\\t// Decrypt range (DecryptRange internally calculates block indices from plaintext offsets)\\n1040\\t\\t// For range: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n1045\\t\\t\\treturn\\n1046\\t\\t}\\n1047\\t\\n1048\\t\\t// Cache Parquet footer if this looks like a footer read\\n1049\\t\\t// Footer is detected by: 1) Reading near end of file, 2) Data ends with \\\"PAR1\\\" magic\\n1050\\t\\tif h.footerCache != nil && end >= plaintextSize-8 && len(plaintext) >= 8 {\\n1051\\t\\t\\tif 10))\\n1061\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", armorMeta.ContentType)\\n1062\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"ETag\\\", fmt.Sprintf(`\\\"%s\\\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\\n1063\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Accept-Ranges\\\", \\\"bytes\\\")\\n1064\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Last-Modified\\\", lastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\\n1065\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Content-Range\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"bytes %d-%d/%d\\\", start, end, plaintextSize))\\n1066\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusPartialContent)\\n1067\\t\\tw.Write(plaintext)\\n1068\\t}\\n1069\\t\\n1070\\t// cacheParquetFooter caches Parquet footer data for faster subsequent reads.\\n1071\\tfunc (h *Handlers) cacheParquetFooter(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, plaintext []byte, plaintextSize int64) {\\n1072\\t\\t// If we got the last 8 bytes, we can determine footer length\\n1073\\t\\tif len(plaintext) == 8 {\\n1074\\t\\t\\t// This is the footer length read - cache a small marker\\n1075\\t\\t\\t// The actual footer will be cached on the next read\\n1076\\t\\t\\treturn\\n1077\\t\\t}\\n1078\\t\\n1079\\t\\t// Check if we have the full footer by verifying PAR1 magic at end\\n1080\\t\\tif len(plaintext) >= 8 && backend.IsParquetFile(plaintext[len(plaintext)-4:]) {\\n1081\\t\\t\\t// We have at least part of the footer\\n1082\\t\\t\\t// Parse footer length from the last 8 bytes if available\\n1083\\t\\t\\tfooterRange, err := backend.GetParquetFooterRange(plaintext[len(plaintext)-8:], plaintextSize)\\n1084\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n1085\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n1086\\t\\t\\t}\\n1087\\t\\n1088\\t\\t\\t// Check if we have the complete footer\\n1089\\t\\t\\tfooterStart := plaintextSize - int64(footerRange.Length) - 8\\n1090\\t\\t\\trequestStart := plaintextSize - int64(len(plaintext))\\n1091\\t\\n1092\\t\\t\\tif requestStart <= footerStart {\\n1093\\t\\t\\t\\t// We have the complete footer, extract and cache plaintext[footerOffset : int(footerOffset)+footerRange.Length+8]\\n1097\\t\\t\\t\\t\\th.footerCache.Set(bucket, key, armorMeta.ETag, footer)\\n1098\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1099\\t\\t\\t}\\n1100\\t\\t}\\n1101\\t}\\n1102\\t\\n1103\\t// checkConditionalRequest evaluates conditional headers and returns the appropriate\\n1104\\t// response status. Returns 0 if the request should proceed normally.\\n1105\\t// Supports: If-Match, If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since, If-Unmodified-Since\\n1106\\tfunc checkConditionalRequest(r *http.Request, etag string, lastModified time.Time) int {\\n1107\\t\\tifMatch := r.Header.Get(\\\"If-Match\\\")\\n1108\\t\\tifNoneMatch := r.Header.Get(\\\"If-None-Match\\\")\\n1109\\t\\tifModifiedSince := r.Header.Get(\\\"If-Modified-Since\\\")\\n1110\\t\\tifUnmodifiedSince := r.Header.Get(\\\"If-Unmodified-Since\\\")\\n1111\\t\\n1112\\t\\t// Normalize ETag (remove quotes if present for comparison)\\n1113\\t\\tnormalizedETag := strings.Trim(etag, `\\\"`)\\n1114\\t\\n1115\\t\\t// If-Match: Return 412 Precondition Failed if ETag doesn't match\\n1116\\t\\tif ifMatch != \\\"\\\" {\\n1117\\t\\t\\t// If-Match can be \\\"*\\\" (match any) or a comma-separated list of ETags\\n1118\\t\\t\\tif ifMatch == \\\"*\\\" {\\n1119\\t\\t\\t\\t// Match any existing resource - proceed\\n1120\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n1121\\t\\t\\t\\t// Parse comma-separated ETags\\n1122\\t\\t\\t\\tetags := strings.Split(ifMatch, \\\",\\\")\\n1123\\t\\t\\t\\tmatched := false\\n1124\\t\\t\\t\\tfor _, e := range etags {\\n1125\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Trim space first, then quotes (order matters for \\\" value\\\" case)\\n1126\\t\\t\\t\\t\\te = strings.Trim(strings.TrimSpace(e), `\\\"`)\\n1127\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif e == normalizedETag {\\n1128\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmatched = true\\n1129\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n1130\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1131\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1132\\t\\t\\t\\tif !matched {\\n1133\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn http.StatusPreconditionFailed\\n1134\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1135\\t\\t\\t}\\n1136\\t\\t}\\n1137\\t\\n1138\\t\\t// If-Unmodified-Since: Return 412 Precondition Failed if modified since date\\n1139\\t\\tif ifUnmodifiedSince != \\\"\\\" {\\n1140\\t\\t\\tif t, err := http.ParseTime(ifUnmodifiedSince); err == nil {\\n1141\\t\\t\\t\\tif lastModified.After(t) {\\n1142\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn http.StatusPreconditionFailed\\n1143\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1144\\t\\t\\t}\\n1145\\t\\t}\\n1146\\t\\n1147\\t\\t// If-None-Match: Return 304 Not Modified if ETag matches\\n1148\\t\\tif ifNoneMatch != \\\"\\\" {\\n1149\\t\\t\\t// If-None-Match can be \\\"*\\\" (match any) or a comma-separated list of ETags\\n1150\\t\\t\\tif ifNoneMatch == \\\"*\\\" {\\n1151\\t\\t\\t\\treturn http.StatusNotModified\\n1152\\t\\t\\t}\\n1153\\t\\t\\t// Parse comma-separated ETags\\n1154\\t\\t\\tetags := strings.Split(ifNoneMatch, \\\",\\\")\\n1155\\t\\t\\tfor _, e := range etags {\\n1156\\t\\t\\t\\t// Trim space first, then quotes (order matters for \\\" value\\\" case)\\n1157\\t\\t\\t\\te = strings.Trim(strings.TrimSpace(e), `\\\"`)\\n1158\\t\\t\\t\\tif e == normalizedETag {\\n1159\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn http.StatusNotModified\\n1160\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1161\\t\\t\\t}\\n1162\\t\\t}\\n1163\\t\\n1164\\t\\t// If-Modified-Since: Return 304 Not Modified if not modified since date\\n1165\\t\\t// Only applies if If-None-Match is not present (per RFC 7232)\\n1166\\t\\tif ifModifiedSince != \\\"\\\" && ifNoneMatch == \\\"\\\" {\\n1167\\t\\t\\tif t, err := http.ParseTime(ifModifiedSince); err == nil {\\n1168\\t\\t\\t\\t// Use >= comparison per RFC 7232\\n1169\\t\\t\\t\\tif !lastModified.After(t) {\\n1170\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn http.StatusNotModified\\n1171\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1172\\t\\t\\t}\\n1173\\t\\t}\\n1174\\t\\n1175\\t\\treturn 0\\n1176\\t}\\n1177\\t\\n1178\\t// parseRangeHeader parses a Range header like \\\"bytes=0-1023\\\".\\n1179\\tfunc parseRangeHeader(header string, totalSize int64) (start, end int64, err error) {\\n1180\\t\\tif !strings.HasPrefix(header, \\\"bytes=\\\") {\\n1181\\t\\t\\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid range format\\\")\\n1182\\t\\t}\\n1183\\t\\n1184\\t\\trangeSpec := strings.TrimPrefix(header, \\\"bytes=\\\")\\n1185\\t\\n1186\\t\\tif strings.Contains(rangeSpec, \\\",\\\") {\\n1187\\t\\t\\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\\\"multiple ranges not supported\\\")\\n1188\\t\\t}\\n1189\\t\\n1190\\t\\tparts := strings.Split(rangeSpec, \\\"-\\\")\\n1191\\t\\tif len(parts) != 2 {\\n1192\\t\\t\\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid range format\\\")\\n1193\\t\\t}\\n1194\\t\\n1195\\t\\tif parts[0] == \\\"\\\" {\\n1196\\t\\t\\t// Suffix range: -500 means last 500 bytes\\n1197\\t\\t\\tsuffix, err := strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\\n1198\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n1199\\t\\t\\t\\treturn 0, 0, err\\n1200\\t\\t\\t}\\n1201\\t\\t\\tstart = totalSize - suffix\\n1202\\t\\t\\tend = totalSize - 1\\n1203\\t\\t} else {\\n1204\\t\\t\\tstart, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[0], 10, 64)\\n1205\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n1206\\t\\t\\t\\treturn 0, 0, err\\n1207\\t\\t\\t}\\n1208\\t\\t\\tif parts[1] == \\\"\\\" {\\n1209\\t\\t\\t\\tend = totalSize - 1\\n1210\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n1211\\t\\t\\t\\tend, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\\n1212\\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n1213\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn 0, 0, err\\n1214\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1215\\t\\t\\t}\\n1216\\t\\t}\\n1217\\t\\n1218\\t\\t// Clamp end per RFC 7233: a range ending beyond the file size is satisfiable\\n1219\\t\\t// as long as start is within bounds. DuckDB httpfs requests aligned blocks\\n1220\\t\\t// (e.g. bytes=0-131071) regardless of actual file size.\\n1221\\t\\tif end >= totalSize {\\n1222\\t\\t\\tend = totalSize - 1\\n1223\\t\\t}\\n1224\\t\\n1225\\t\\tif start < 0 || start >= totalSize || end < start {\\n1226\\t\\t\\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\\\"range out of bounds\\\")\\n1227\\t\\t}\\n1228\\t\\n1229\\t\\treturn start, end, nil\\n1230\\t}\\n1231\\t\\n1232\\t// HeadObject handles S3 HeadObject.\\n1233\\tfunc (h *Handlers) HeadObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\\n1234\\t\\tctx := r.Context()\\n1235\\t\\n1236\\t\\t// Fast path: serve from the in-memory manifest index when available,\\n1237\\t\\t// avoiding a B2 HeadObject round-trip entirely.\\n1238\\t\\tif h.manifest != nil {\\n1239\\t\\t\\tif entry, ok := h.manifest.Lookup(bucket, key); ok {\\n1240\\t\\t\\t\\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, entry.ETag, entry.LastModified); status != 0 {\\n1241\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\\n1242\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"ETag\\\", fmt.Sprintf(`\\\"%s\\\"`, entry.ETag))\\n1243\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Last-Modified\\\", entry.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\\n1244\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(status)\\n1245\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n1246\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"PreconditionFailed\\\", \\\"Precondition failed\\\", status)\\n1247\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1248\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n1249\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1250\\t\\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Content-Length\\\", strconv.FormatInt(entry.PlaintextSize, 10))\\n1251\\t\\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", entry.ContentType)\\n1252\\t\\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"ETag\\\", fmt.Sprintf(`\\\"%s\\\"`, entry.ETag))\\n1253\\t\\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Accept-Ranges\\\", \\\"bytes\\\")\\n1254\\t\\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Last-Modified\\\", entry.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\\n1255\\t\\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\\n1256\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n1257\\t\\t\\t}\\n1258\\t\\t}\\n1259\\t\\n1260\\t\\t// Manifest miss or disabled: fall back to a B2 HeadObject call.\\n1261\\t\\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\\n1262\\t\\tinfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey)\\n1263\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n1264\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"NoSuchKey\\\", \\\"Object not found\\\", 404)\\n1265\\t\\t\\treturn\\n1266\\t\\t}\\n1267\\t\\n1268\\t\\t// Determine ETag and content info based on encryption status\\n1269\\t\\tvar etag string\\n1270\\t\\tvar contentLength int64\\n1271\\t\\tvar contentType string\\n1272\\t\\tif info.IsARMOREncrypted {\\n1273\\t\\t\\tif am, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); ok {\\n1274\\t\\t\\t\\tetag = am.ETag\\n1275\\t\\t\\t\\tcontentLength = am.PlaintextSize\\n1276\\t\\t\\t\\tcontentType = am.ContentType\\n1277\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n1278\\t\\t\\t\\tetag = info.ETag\\n1279\\t\\t\\t\\tcontentLength = info.Size\\n1280\\t\\t\\t\\tcontentType = info.ContentType\\n1281\\t\\t\\t}\\n1282\\t\\t} else {\\n1283\\t\\t\\tetag = info.ETag\\n1284\\t\\t\\tcontentLength = info.Size\\n1285\\t\\t\\tcontentType = info.ContentType\\n1286\\t\\t}\\n1287\\t\\n1288\\t\\t// Check conditional request headers\\n1289\\t\\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, etag, info.LastModified); status != 0 {\\n1290\\t\\t\\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\\n1291\\t\\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"ETag\\\", fmt.Sprintf(`\\\"%s\\\"`, etag))\\n1292\\t\\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Last-Modified\\\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\\n1293\\t\\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(status)\\n1294\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n1295\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"PreconditionFailed\\\", \\\"Precondition failed\\\", status)\\n1296\\t\\t\\t}\\n1297\\t\\t\\treturn\\n1298\\t\\t}\\n1299\\t\\n1300\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Content-Length\\\", strconv.FormatInt(contentLength, 10))\\n1301\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", contentType)\\n1302\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"ETag\\\", fmt.Sprintf(`\\\"%s\\\"`, etag))\\n1303\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Accept-Ranges\\\", \\\"bytes\\\")\\n1304\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Last-Modified\\\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\\n1305\\t\\n1306\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\\n1307\\t}\\n1308\\t\\n1309\\t// DeleteObject handles S3 DeleteObject.\\n1310\\tfunc (h *Handlers) DeleteObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\\n1311\\t\\tctx := r.Context()\\n1312\\t\\n1313\\t\\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\\n1314\\t\\tif err := h.backend.Delete(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey); err != nil {\\n1315\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Failed to delete: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n1316\\t\\t\\treturn\\n1317\\t\\t}\\n1318\\t\\n1319\\t\\t// Remove from manifest\\n1320\\t\\tif h.manifest != nil {\\n1321\\t\\t\\th.manifest.RecordDelete(bucket, key)\\n1322\\t\\t}\\n1323\\t\\n1324\\t\\t// Invalidate metadata cache and list cache\\n1325\\t\\th.cache.Delete(bucket, key)\\n1326\\t\\tif h.listCache != nil {\\n1327\\t\\t\\th.listCache.InvalidatePrefix(bucket, path.Dir(key)+\\\"/\\\")\\n1328\\t\\t}\\n1329\\t\\n1330\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\\n1331\\t}\\n1332\\t\\n1333\\t// CopyObject handles S3 CopyObject with DEK re-wrapping for ARMOR-encrypted objects.\\n1334\\t// This supports:\\n1335\\t// - Renaming files (same bucket, different key)\\n1336\\t// - Copying files (potentially different bucket)\\n1337\\t// - Key rotation (re-wraps DEK with current MEK)\\n1338\\tfunc (h *Handlers) CopyObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, dstBucket, dstKey string) {\\n1339\\t\\tctx := r.Context()\\n1340\\t\\n1341\\t\\t// Parse copy source header\\n1342\\t\\tcopySource := r.Header.Get(\\\"x-amz-copy-source\\\")\\n1343\\t\\tif copySource == \\\"\\\" {\\n1344\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InvalidRequest\\\", \\\"Missing x-amz-copy-source header\\\", 400)\\n1345\\t\\t\\treturn\\n1346\\t\\t}\\n1347\\t\\n1348\\t\\t// Parse source bucket and key\\n1349\\t\\t// Format: /bucket/key or bucket/key\\n1350\\t\\tsrcBucket, srcKey := parseCopySource(copySource)\\n1351\\t\\tif srcBucket == \\\"\\\" || srcKey == \\\"\\\" 404)\\n1364\\t\\t\\treturn\\n1365\\t\\t}\\n1366\\t\\n1367\\t\\t// Check metadata directive\\n1368\\t\\tmetadataDirective := r.Header.Get(\\\"x-amz-metadata-directive\\\")\\n1369\\t\\treplaceMetadata := metadataDirective == \\\"REPLACE\\\"\\n1370\\t\\n1371\\t\\t// Build response XML structure\\n1372\\t\\ttype CopyObjectResult struct {\\n1373\\t\\t\\tXMLName      xml.Name `xml:\\\"CopyObjectResult\\\"`\\n1374\\t\\t\\tLastModified string   `xml:\\\"LastModified\\\"`\\n1375\\t\\t\\tETag         string   `xml:\\\"ETag\\\"`\\n1376\\t\\t}\\n1377\\t\\n1378\\t\\t// Handle !ok {\\n1383\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", \\\"Failed to parse ARMOR metadata\\\", 500)\\n1384\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n1385\\t\\t\\t}\\n1386\\t\\n1387\\t\\t\\t// Get the source MEK 500)\\n1398\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n1399\\t\\t\\t}\\n1400\\t\\n1401\\t\\t\\t// Get the destination MEK for the target key\\n1402\\t\\t\\tdstMEK, dstKeyID, err := 500)\\n1405\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n1406\\t\\t\\t}\\n1407\\t\\n1408\\t\\t\\t// Re-wrap DEK with destination MEK (handles key rotation and cross-prefix copy)\\n1409\\t\\t\\twrappedDEK, err := 500)\\n1412\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n1413\\t\\t\\t}\\n1414\\t\\n1415\\t\\t\\t// Build new metadata with re-wrapped DEK and destination key ID\\n1416\\t\\t\\tnewMeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\\n1417\\t\\t\\t\\tVersion:       armorMeta.Version,\\n1418\\t\\t\\t\\tBlockSize:     armorMeta.BlockSize,\\n1419\\t\\t\\t\\tPlaintextSize: armorMeta.PlaintextSize,\\n1420\\t\\t\\t\\tContentType:   armorMeta.ContentType,\\n1421\\t\\t\\t\\tIV:            armorMeta.IV,\\n1422\\t\\t\\t\\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\\n1423\\t\\t\\t\\tPlaintextSHA:  armorMeta.PlaintextSHA,\\n1424\\t\\t\\t\\tETag:          armorMeta.ETag,\\n1425\\t\\t\\t\\tKeyID:         dstKeyID,\\n1426\\t\\t\\t}).ToMetadata()\\n1427\\t\\n1428\\t\\t\\t// Handle REPLACE directive - copy custom metadata headers from request\\n1429\\t\\t\\tif replaceMetadata {\\n1430\\t\\t\\t\\t// Check for new content-type in request\\n1431\\t\\t\\t\\tif ct := r.Header.Get(\\\"Content-Type\\\"); ct != \\\"\\\" {\\n1432\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tnewMeta[\\\"x-amz-meta-armor-content-type\\\"] = \\\"X-Amz-Meta-\\\") && !strings.HasPrefix(k, \\\"X-Amz-Meta-Armor-\\\") {\\n1437\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tnewMeta[strings.ToLower(k)] = v[0]\\n1438\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1439\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1440\\t\\t\\t}\\n1441\\t\\n1442\\t\\t\\t// Perform server-side copy with updated metadata\\n1443\\t\\t\\tif err := h.backend.Copy(ctx, srcBucket, srcPrefixedKey, dstBucket, dstPrefixedKey, newMeta, true); err != nil {\\n1444\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Copy failed: %v\\\", err), 500)\\n1445\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n1446\\t\\t\\t}\\n1447\\t\\n1448\\t\\t\\t// Record provenance for the copy\\n1449\\t\\t\\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(dstKey) {\\n1450\\t\\t\\t\\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, dstKey, armorMeta.PlaintextSHA, \\\"copy\\\")\\n1451\\t\\t\\t}\\n1452\\t\\n1453\\t\\t\\t// Invalidate cache for destination\\n1454\\t\\t\\th.cache.Delete(dstBucket, dstKey)\\n1455\\t\\t\\th.footerCache.Delete(dstBucket, dstKey)\\n1456\\t\\n1457\\t\\t\\t// Return success response\\n1458\\t\\t\\tresult := armorMeta.ETag),\\n1461\\t\\t\\t}\\n1462\\t\\n1463\\t\\t\\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\\n1464\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n1465\\t\\t\\t\\th.writeError(w, \\\"InternalError\\\", \\\"Failed to \\\"application/xml\\\")\\n1470\\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\\n1471\\t\\t\\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\\\"1.0\\\"
